50 cool original artist names derived from greek or latin with their meaning in parentheses next to it

1. Ophelia (help)
2. Anima (soul)
3. Aquila (eagle)
4. Calypso (to cover, conceal)
5. Aegis (protection)
6. Eurydice (wide justice)
7. Daphne (laurel tree)
8. Aurora (dawn)
9. Castor (to shine)
10. Euterpe (delight)
11. Cygnus (swan)
12. Galatea (to milk)
13. Atalanta (balancing scale)
14. Aeolus (wind)
15. Helios (sun)
16. Arion (tearful)
17. Clio (glory)
18. Gaia (earth)
19. Apollo (destroyer)
20. Demeter (earth mother)
21. Hermes (guardian)
22. Narcissus (sleep)
23. Persephone (death bringer)
24. Proteus (first)
25. Bacchus (god of wine)
26. Calliope (exquisite voice)
27. Harpocrates (god of silence)
28. Juno (queen of gods)
29. Meliora (better things)
30. Nemesis (revenge)
31. Phoebe (bright)
32. Uranus (heaven)
33. Vulcan (god of fire)
34. Zephyr (west wind)
35. Tantalus (to torment)
36. Gaetan (from Gaeta, Italy)
37. Adrastea (the unyielding)
38. Alecta (strength)
39. Atlas (support)
40. Bellona (war goddess)
41. Charis (grace)
42. Lyris (lyric)
43. Manu (god of creation)
44. Medusa (guardian)
45. Metis (wisdom)
46. Orpheus (bright)
47. Pandora (all gifted)
48. Sidonie (from Sidon)
49. Tanith (goddess of fire)
50. Terpsichore (enjoyment of dance)
original ones

1. Astraell (derived from "astra," meaning star, and "ell," a suffix meaning "of the")
2. Ethereus (derived from "ethereal," meaning celestial or heavenly)
3. Novare (derived from "nova," meaning a sudden brightening of a star, and "-are," a suffix meaning "pertaining to")
4. Vespera (derived from "vesper," meaning evening or the evening star)
5. Luminus (meaning bright or shining)
6. Solaria (derived from "solar," meaning relating to the sun)
7. Solstice (derived from "sol," meaning sun, and "stices," meaning standing still)
8. Helianthe (derived from "helios," meaning sun, and "anthe," meaning flower)
9. Astris (derived from "astro," meaning star)
10. Nebulae (derived from "nebula," meaning a cloud of gas and dust in outer space)
11. Tenebris (meaning darkness)
12. Aetheria (derived from "aether," meaning the upper atmosphere or sky)
13. Ignis (meaning fire)
14. Umbrae (meaning shade or shadow)
15. Zenitha (derived from "zenith," meaning the highest point in the sky)
16. Arcanae (meaning secrets or mysteries)
17. Noctilucent (derived from "noctilucent," meaning shining at night)
18. Aurorae (derived from "aurora," meaning dawn or the northern lights)
19. Galaxia (derived from "galaxy," meaning a system of millions or billions of stars)
20. Umbrella (meaning protection or shelter)
21. Luxuria (meaning luxury or indulgence)
22. Nebulae (derived from "nebula," meaning a cloud of gas and dust in outer space)
23. Orionis (derived from "Orion," meaning a constellation of stars)
24. Nadirin (derived from "nadir," meaning the lowest point in the sky)
25. Valora (meaning value or worth)
26. Starflame (derived from "star" and "flame")
27. Celestia (meaning celestial)
28. Sidereum (derived from "sidereal," meaning relating to the stars)
29. Ignition (meaning the act or process of starting something on fire)
30. Cratere (derived from "crater," meaning a large, bowl-shaped depression on the surface of the moon or a planet)
31. Lunaris (derived from "lunar," meaning relating to the moon)
32. Stellaris (meaning relating to the stars)
33. Solara (meaning relating to the sun)
34. Caelum (meaning sky or heaven)
35. Ashenelle (derived from "ashen," meaning pale gray and "elle," meaning feminine)
36. Axiomus (meaning a self-evident or universally accepted truth)
37. Corvus (meaning crow)
38. Pensato (meaning thoughtful or reflective)
39. Chromatico (derived from "chromatic," meaning relating to colors)
40. Oculus (meaning eye)
41. Vortexia (derived from "vortex," meaning a mass of spinning fluid, gas, or air)
42. Voltaire (meaning high-flying or flighty)
43. Gossamira (derived from "gossamer," meaning a fine, filmy, delicate fabric)
44. Bellatrix (derived from "Bellatrix," meaning a star in the constellation of Orion)
45. Elipsion (derived from "eclipse," meaning the obscuring of a celestial body)
46. Fenris (meaning wolf or monster)
47. Accendio (meaning to ignite or set on fire)
48. Introspecto (meaning introspective or self-reflective)
49. Xanthea (derived from "xanth," meaning yellow or golden)
50. Oceania (meaning relating to the ocean or sea)
